entries.title: Colony Entry Turns Setup Into Active Rule Field

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen early and middle Culling Game colony action through Tokyo No. 2 Hakari/Kashimo material.

content.fact: Once characters enter the Culling Game colonies, the conflict shifts from preparation into an active rule field where points, barriers, and player choices constrain survival.
content.injection: During colony scenes, write the Culling Game as an active rules environment, not just a backdrop for ordinary fights.
constraints: Keep late convergence, Gojo unsealing, and Shinjuku outcomes gated to later decks.
antiLore: Do not treat colony entry as reversible or consequence-free travel.

sourceInfo.title: Culling Game
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Points And Added Rules Define Survival Math

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen early and middle Culling Game colony action through Tokyo No. 2 Hakari/Kashimo material.

content.fact: Culling Game points and added rules define survival choices, negotiation leverage, and whether players can help one another without killing.
content.injection: When points are active in Context, make point totals and rule additions matter to tactical choices and moral pressure.
constraints: Do not skip to later rule additions from the convergence deck unless that deck is loaded.
antiLore: Do not make Culling Game points decorative bookkeeping with no scene effect.

sourceInfo.title: Culling Game
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Culling Game Players Are A Mixed Participant Pool

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen early and middle Culling Game colony action through Tokyo No. 2 Hakari/Kashimo material.

content.fact: The Culling Game participant pool mixes modern awakened players, reincarnated sorcerers, and forced or pressured participants with different motives.
content.injection: In colony scenes, vary player motives and time-origin assumptions instead of treating all participants as the same type of curse user.
constraints: Do not reveal late convergence identity twists or final player outcomes from this deck.
antiLore: Do not flatten Culling Game players into a single faction with shared goals.

sourceInfo.title: Culling Game
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Colony Separation Makes Communication Unreliable

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen early and middle Culling Game colony action through Tokyo No. 2 Hakari/Kashimo material.

content.fact: The main group is split across colonies, so information, points, and safety cannot be assumed to move cleanly between Yuji, Megumi, Yuta, and Hakari scenes.
content.injection: During active colony scenes, preserve split-team uncertainty and avoid giving every character complete knowledge of other colonies.
constraints: Let later decks handle communication breakthroughs and convergence.
antiLore: Do not make colony teams instantly coordinate as if all fights are in one shared room.

sourceInfo.title: Culling Game
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.
